assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has created a foreign-currency-denominated intragroup receivable/payable between group entities with different functional currencies.
The related exchange differences on that intragroup monetary item are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised asset, so the fair value hedge model is the most direct hedge accounting model to consider for its foreign currency risk in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also provides a cash flow hedge model for recognised assets or liabilities whose risk exposure could affect profit or loss, making it the other hedge accounting model to consider.
